results.question: Show all students
results.baseline_sql: SELECT * FROM students;
results.system_sql: SELECT * FROM students;

results.question: List all courses
results.baseline_sql: SELECT * FROM courses;
results.system_sql: SELECT * FROM courses;

results.question: Top scoring student
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, MAX(s.marks) AS top_score
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY s.id
ORDER BY top_score DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, MAX(sc.marks) AS top_score
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department;

results.question: Show student name with course and marks
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title, s.marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title, s.marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: List students and their courses
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name AS student_name, c.title AS course_title
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name AS student_name, c.title AS course_title
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: Average marks per student
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY s.id;

results.question: Top 3 students by marks
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, s.id, MAX(s.marks) AS max_marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
WHERE sc.course_id IN (
  SELECT course_id
  FROM courses
  GROUP BY code
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
GROUP BY s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, s.id
ORDER BY max_marks DESC
LIMIT 3;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, s.id, COUNT(*) AS rank
FROM scores s
JOIN students st ON s.student_id = st.id
WHERE s.course_id IN (
    SELECT course_id
    FROM courses
    GROUP BY code
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
GROUP BY s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, s.id
ORDER BY rank DESC;

results.question: student_name instead of name
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.student_name, c.title, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.email, s.age, s.department, c.title 
FROM students s 
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id 
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: student_scores instead of scores
results.baseline_sql:
CREATE TABLE student_scores (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  student_id INT NOT NULL,
  course_id INT NOT NULL,
  marks INT NOT NULL,
  exam_date DATE NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (student_id) REFERENCES students(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (course_id) REFERENCES courses(id)
);
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, c.title, s.age, s.department, s.id AS student_id, c.id AS course_id, s.id AS student_scores
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: Show best student
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, AVG(s.scores.marks) AS avg_marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY s.id
ORDER BY avg_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, c.title, s.marks, s.exam_date
FROM scores s
JOIN students s ON s.student_id = s.id
JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE s.id = (SELECT MAX(s2.id) FROM scores s2 WHERE s.student_id = s2.student_id AND s.id <> s2.id)
ORDER BY s.marks DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: Top performer
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, c.title, s.marks, s.exam_date
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
ORDER BY s.marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, c.title, s.age, s.department, ssc.marks, ssc.exam_date
FROM students s
JOIN scores ssc ON s.id = ssc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON ssc.course_id = c.id
ORDER BY ssc.marks DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: High scoring students
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, c.title, s.id
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks >= 80
ORDER BY sc.marks DESC;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, c.title
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks >= 80;

results.question: List student emails with course names and marks above average
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.email, c.title, s.marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks > (
    SELECT AVG(s2.marks)
    FROM scores s2
    JOIN students s3 ON s2.student_id = s3.id
    JOIN courses c4 ON s2.course_id = c4.id
    WHERE s3.id = s.id
)
ORDER BY s.email, c.title;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.email, c.title, s.marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks > (
    SELECT AVG(s2.marks)
    FROM scores s2
    JOIN students s3 ON s2.student_id = s3.id
    JOIN courses c4 ON s2.course_id = c4.id
    WHERE s3.id = s.id
);

results.question: Students who performed better than average in each course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks > (
  SELECT AVG(sc2.marks)
  FROM scores sc2
  JOIN students st2 ON sc2.student_id = st2.id
  JOIN courses c2 ON sc2.course_id = c2.id
  WHERE s.id = st2.id AND c.id = c2.id
);
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title 
FROM students s 
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id 
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id 
WHERE sc.marks > (SELECT AVG(sc2.marks) FROM scores sc2 WHERE sc.course_id = sc2.course_id);

results.question: Find students who scored above average in at least 2 courses
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks > (
    SELECT AVG(sc2.marks)
    FROM scores sc2
    JOIN courses c2 ON sc2.course_id = c2.id
    WHERE sc2.student_id = s.id
)
GROUP BY s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks > (
    SELECT AVG(sc2.marks)
    FROM scores sc2
    JOIN courses c2 ON sc2.course_id = c2.id
    WHERE sc2.student_id = s.id
);

results.question: Top department based on average marks
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT department, AVG(marks) AS avg_marks
FROM scores
JOIN students ON scores.student_id = students.id
JOIN courses ON scores.course_id = courses.id
GROUP BY department
ORDER BY avg_marks DESC;
results.system_sql:
SELECT department, AVG(marks) AS avg_marks
FROM scores
JOIN students ON scores.student_id = students.id
JOIN courses ON scores.course_id = courses.id
GROUP BY department;

results.system_sql:
SELECT name

results.question: course score data
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT c.title, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, s.id AS student_id,
       scores.marks, scores.exam_date
FROM courses c
JOIN students s ON scores.course_id = c.id
WHERE scores.student_id IS NOT NULL;

results.question: List students whose average marks are above the overall average
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY s.id
HAVING AVG(s.marks) > (SELECT AVG(sc2.marks) FROM scores sc2 JOIN students s2 ON sc2.student_id = s2.id);
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY s.id
HAVING AVG(s.marks) > (SELECT AVG(sc2.marks) FROM scores sc2 JOIN students s2 ON sc2.student_id = s2.id);

results.question: Show course-wise toppers with student names
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT c.title, s.name
FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
GROUP BY c.id
ORDER BY s.marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT c.title, s.name
FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
GROUP BY c.id
ORDER BY s.marks DESC;

results.question: Count students scoring above 90 in each course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT c.title, COUNT(*) AS count
FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
GROUP BY c.id
HAVING AVG(s.marks) > 90;
results.system_sql:
SELECT c.code, c.title, COUNT(*) AS count
FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
WHERE s.marks > 90
GROUP BY c.code, c.title;
